published_prior_art.cite: standard fixed-margin 2x2 odds-ratio algebra; not a flagship novelty claim

repo_prior_art.cite: closest: pid_ordinal_principal_strata_v1 and pid_rdd_manipulation_bounded_v1 are adjacent LP/budgeted-selection proposals, not duplicates

published_prior_art.cite: Lee2009SampleSelection, HuberMellace2015SharpSelection, LaffersNedela2017Sensitivity, Semenova2025GeneralizedLee, Okamoto2025RobustifyLee do not state this alpha-coupled finite-(Gamma,kappa) selected-mixture frontier
finite sensitivity tightening is familiar, but the proposed equality-case classification for the Lee alpha-profile is not found in the checked papers

ShepherdGilbertJemiaiRotnitzky2006/JemiaiRotnitzkyShepherdGilbert2007 and HonoreHu2020Selection are real comparators, but the endpoint functionals v_SGJR and v_HH are drafter-defined rather than published endpoint problems

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 3
novelty_flags.one_line: The SGJR/JR comparison is inaccurate: ShepherdGilbertJemiaiRotnitzky2006 and JemiaiRotnitzkyShepherdGilbert2007 impose principal-stratum/monotonicity modelling, not the margin-free armwise support problem v_SGJR defined here.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 3
novelty_flags.one_line: The HonoreHu2020Selection comparison is overstated: that paper studies selection without exclusion in a sample-selection model and does not publish the OR-capped Lee principal-share support functional v_HH used here.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The principal-strata sharp-bounds survey should add closer truncation-by-death/principal-effect bounds such as Imai2008 and Chiba2012, not rely only on ZhangRubin2003 plus sensitivity papers.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 3
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=field below novelty_target=flagship; the iff frontier is not yet between two actual named published estimators/strategies because the load-bearing endpoint problems are reconstructed by the proposal.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 3
soundness_flags.one_line: Delta_HH is not well-posed as written: R(h) is called a nonempty set of principal-strata refinements, but if it is truly a principal refinement then the common-alpha equalities already hold; if they fail, R(h) may be empty and the gap is undefined.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace v_SGJR with an exact finite-binary specialization of the published SGJR/JR assumptions, including their monotonicity/principal-stratum restrictions, or rename it as a new armwise beta/psi outer benchmark not claimed to be published.
Redefine Delta_HH directly over exposed no-exclusion table coordinates, with an explicit convention for empty compatibility sets, so positive incompatibility is mathematically meaningful.
Add Imai2008 and Chiba2012 to the related-work comparison for principal-strata sharp bounds under truncation by death/sample selection.
Correct the HonoreHu2020Selection bibliography DOI to 10.3982/ECTA16481.

recommended_next_step: Patch Conjecture 3 so the threshold is against actual published endpoint functionals, then rerun novelty review against the flagship floor.
